heading: How Performance Advisor Suggests and Ranks Indexes
body:
The Performance Advisor monitors queries that take longer than `100` milliseconds to
execute and groups these queries into common [query shape](manual/reference/glossary). The Performance Advisor calculates the inefficiency of each query shape by
considering the following aggregated metrics from queries which match
the shape:

- Amount of time spent executing the query.

- Number of documents scanned.

- Number of documents returned.

- Average object size.

To establish recommended indexes, the Performance Advisor uses these metrics in a
formula to calculate the **Impact**, or performance improvement
that creating an index matching that query shape would cause. When the
Performance Advisor suggests indexes, it ranks the indexes by their
**Impact**. The **Impact** is determined by the total
wasted bytes read.
